_comment: Published Vincio performance & quality SLOs. Each SLO is a public promise; `enforced_by` names the budget key in budgets.json that gates it in CI. The published `threshold` is the promise to users; the CI budget is held at least as strict, so a build that passes the gate necessarily honors the SLO. tests/test_slos.py verifies that invariant. Measured on the deterministic offline suite (mock provider); see benchmarks/METHODOLOGY.md.

slos.statement: Cold context compilation completes within 300 ms at p95.
slos.rationale: Compilation is on the request hot path; the budget gates 250 ms to leave headroom under the promise.

slos.statement: The content-addressed compile cache yields at least a 1.5x speedup over the cold path.
slos.rationale: Repeat compilations of unchanged inputs must amortize; protects the caching contract.

slos.statement: Cold context compilation completes within 500 ms at p99.
slos.rationale: The tail of the compile hot path is gated, not just the median; the budget holds 350 ms for headroom.

slos.statement: A warm compile (content-addressed cache hit) is dominated by the cache lookup, completing well under a millisecond on the reference corpus.
slos.rationale: The warm hot path must stay allocation-free and sub-millisecond; the budget gates 5 ms for order-of-magnitude regression detection on shared runners.

slos.statement: Batched candidate scoring produces identical scores to the per-candidate loop.
slos.rationale: The single-pass (NumPy-optional) scorer is a hot-path accelerator; it must never change which context is selected.

slos.statement: The compiled-prompt render program produces byte-identical output to compiling from scratch.
slos.rationale: Reusing the rendered stable prefix must not alter the prompt; correctness is the precondition for the speedup.

slos.statement: Reusing an unchanged candidate set compiles the same context as a cold compile for a new query.
slos.rationale: The warm candidate arena reuses only query-independent preparation; the selected context must be identical.

slos.statement: Streaming compilation emits the stable prefix before any candidate is scored.
slos.rationale: Overlapping the prefix with selection requires the prefix to be available first; a downstream consumer can begin while scoring runs.

slos.statement: Speculative prefetch warms the query embedding so retrieval's embed is a cache hit.
slos.rationale: Prefetch is only worthwhile if the warmed vector is the one retrieval needs; otherwise it is wasted work.

slos.statement: A declared per-app memory ceiling is held by slimming the packet and evicting the lowest-utility evidence.
slos.rationale: A resident-memory budget is only a guarantee if it is actually enforced when the context would exceed it.

slos.statement: The compiled context packet's estimated resident footprint on the reference corpus stays under 6 KB.
slos.rationale: A footprint regression gate catches packet bloat; the budget holds 5 KB, below the published ceiling.

slos.statement: Retrieval over an in-memory index returns within 150 ms at p95.
slos.rationale: Retrieval fan-out is concurrent; the budget gates 100 ms for order-of-magnitude regression detection.

slos.statement: A cached end-to-end run completes within 300 ms at p50 (offline).
slos.rationale: End-to-end median latency for a warm pipeline; budget gates 250 ms.

slos.statement: The engine sustains at least 50 concurrent runs per second (offline).
slos.rationale: Async hot paths must not serialize under concurrency.

slos.statement: The first streamed token is emitted before the run completes (true TTFT < full).
slos.rationale: Streaming must deliver incremental tokens, not buffer the whole response.

slos.statement: The context compiler reduces evidence tokens by at least 20% versus naive stuffing.
slos.rationale: Budgeting and scoring exist to cut tokens; the floor protects the core value proposition.

slos.statement: The permissioned tool runtime adds at most 50 ms p50 overhead per call.
slos.rationale: Validation, permission checks, and sanitization must stay cheap relative to tool work.

slos.statement: Default hybrid retrieval achieves at least 0.80 recall@3 on the reference corpus.
slos.rationale: Grounding quality depends on recall; gated per-mode as well.

slos.statement: Self-correction recovers every recoverable malformed output within its cycle bound.
slos.rationale: Structured-output reliability is a headline guarantee.

slos.statement: Prompt-injection detection rate is at least 0.80 on the SecurityBench attack set.
slos.rationale: Injection defense must catch the bulk of known patterns.

slos.statement: Injection false-positive rate stays at or below 0.20 on benign inputs.
slos.rationale: Defense that blocks legitimate traffic is unusable.

slos.statement: PII detection covers at least 0.80 of the SecurityBench PII set.
slos.rationale: Redaction policies depend on detection coverage.

slos.statement: A consumed MCP tool's input schema is preserved exactly (fidelity 1.0).
slos.rationale: Validation and constrained decoding depend on the discovered schema matching the server's.

slos.statement: A budget-bounded crew delegated over A2A always terminates.
slos.rationale: Termination guarantees must hold across the protocol boundary, not just in-process.

slos.statement: Progressive disclosure keeps off-topic skill bodies out of the budget (>= 0.5 token savings).
slos.rationale: Skills must cost context only when used; otherwise loading many skills defeats budgeting.

slos.statement: Trajectory and tool-use metrics agree with labeled agent traces at 0.90 or higher.
slos.rationale: Trajectory scores must track ground truth, or trajectory-gated releases would be meaningless.

slos.statement: Trajectory evaluation flags runs that final-output-only evaluation passes.
slos.rationale: A wrong path with a right-looking answer must be catchable; output-only eval cannot see it.

slos.statement: The user simulator is seed-deterministic: same seed produces the same conversation.
slos.rationale: Simulated multi-turn cases are only usable as CI goldens if they replay identically.

slos.statement: Drift detection catches at least 85% of known metric regressions.
slos.rationale: Continuous quality depends on catching real regressions early.

slos.statement: Drift detection raises no alarm on at least 90% of stable windows.
slos.rationale: False drift alarms erode trust and trigger needless rollbacks.

slos.statement: Cohen's κ between an agreeing judge and human labels is tracked at 0.75 or higher before the judge earns gating weight.
slos.rationale: An LLM judge should only gate CI once it has demonstrably agreed with people.

slos.statement: A judge ensemble earns CI-gating weight only after its Cohen's κ against human labels clears the trust threshold.
slos.rationale: A panel can be confident and still be wrong; only κ-validation against people licenses it to block a release.

slos.statement: When a gate regresses, Shapley counterfactual replay accounts for the full score drop across the changed components.
slos.rationale: Explaining a regression — not just flagging it — requires the per-component credit to sum exactly to the observed delta.

slos.statement: Adaptive eval sampling reaches the same gate verdict as the exhaustive run for strictly fewer samples.
slos.rationale: Spending the eval budget where the variance is must converge on the same decision — cheaper, never different.

slos.statement: Every batched request is reconciled by custom id; results are never silently dropped.
slos.rationale: Latency-tolerant batch work must return a result for every request — losing one corrupts evals and bulk extraction.

slos.statement: A tripped circuit recovers through a half-open probe once the provider is healthy again.
slos.rationale: A breaker that opens but never closes turns a transient outage into a permanent one.

slos.statement: Provider-aware prompt caching achieves at least a 50% input-token hit rate on a warm, stable prefix.
slos.rationale: Stable system/tool/context prefixes are the bulk of input tokens; caching them is the single biggest cost lever.

slos.statement: Cost rolled up by tenant/feature equals the sum of the attributed per-call costs.
slos.rationale: FinOps decisions and per-tenant budgets are only trustworthy if attribution is exact, not estimated.

slos.statement: The reflective optimizer promotes a candidate that beats the baseline on a failing prompt, within its rollout budget.
slos.rationale: Failure-driven reflection must produce a real improvement, not just churn, to justify the rollouts it spends.

slos.statement: A distilled student is promoted into the cascade only when it holds the teacher's quality; a quality drop is rejected.
slos.rationale: Turning traces into cheaper inference must never silently trade away accuracy; the gate is the whole point.

slos.statement: Learned prompt compression hits its token budget while preserving the cited-fact set.
slos.rationale: A compressor that drops the evidence an answer cites trades tokens for hallucination; faithfulness is the floor.

slos.statement: Matryoshka embeddings at full output dimension hold at least 0.80 recall@3 on the reference corpus.
slos.rationale: MRL is only useful if the full-dimension vector is a no-regression baseline for the truncated ones.

slos.statement: Matryoshka truncation to one-eighth of the base dimension still holds at least 0.80 recall@3.
slos.rationale: The point of MRL is shrinking storage/latency without giving up recall; the truncated floor proves the trade is real.

slos.statement: Unified text+image retrieval achieves at least 0.80 recall@3 over the multimodal evidence set.
slos.rationale: Multimodal embeddings only help if image-derived evidence is retrievable in the same space as text.

slos.statement: The OWASP/NIST/MITRE compliance mapping covers at least 70% of catalog controls from the live configuration.
slos.rationale: Governance evidence is only credible if most framework controls are backed by a configured or measured Vincio control.

slos.statement: Right-to-erasure removes exactly the chunks a source contributed, across every index.
slos.rationale: A GDPR erasure that misses chunks is a compliance failure; the count must match the lineage exactly.

slos.statement: Non-English PII locale packs detect at least 80% of national identifiers in their locales.
slos.rationale: An English-only PII control under-reports risk everywhere else; the locale packs must actually fire.

slos.statement: The advertised Budget is a hard cap: a run exceeding max_cost_usd fails instead of overspending.
slos.rationale: A budget that is silently ignored is not a budget; the cap must bite on the single-shot run path.

slos.statement: An unknown model warns instead of silently billing $0.
slos.rationale: Silent $0 pricing hides real spend and capability gaps; unknown models must be observable.

slos.statement: Same-topic evidence that disagrees on a numeric/date value is flagged as a conflict.
slos.rationale: A negation-only conflict check misses every value disagreement; the compiler must catch them.

slos.statement: The streaming and non-streaming run paths produce the same answer.
slos.rationale: Two divergent pipelines drift as features land; one inner pipeline keeps them identical.

slos.statement: A cancelled run is still fully recorded on the audit chain.
slos.rationale: Cancellation must not lose the trail; the run epilogue audits even on cancel.

slos.statement: Auto-promotion is gated on statistical significance, not a point estimate.
slos.rationale: A point-estimate gate promotes noise; promotions must be defensible at a confidence level.

slos.statement: BM25 search scans inverted posting lists, not every document per query term.
slos.rationale: Rescanning every document per term is quadratic at corpus scale; posting lists keep it sub-linear.

slos.statement: Failover skips a model that cannot serve the request instead of returning a wrong answer.
slos.rationale: A capability-mismatched substitution (e.g. a vision request to a text-only model) silently drops content; the guard refuses it.

slos.statement: A retired/removed-model failure is classified as a terminal lifecycle error, not a transient outage.
slos.rationale: A retired-model 404 must surface 'rotate now' rather than being buried in 'all providers failed'.

slos.statement: The registry-backed router selects the cheapest capable model per request.
slos.rationale: Routing by cost over capable candidates is the FinOps win; an incapable cheaper model is never chosen.

slos.statement: Google/Vertex batch execution bills at the half-cost batch tier, at parity with OpenAI/Anthropic.
slos.rationale: Batch parity across providers keeps the eval/regression workloads the swap gate leans on at half cost everywhere.

slos.statement: A model swap that significantly regresses quality is blocked by the swap gate; a safe one passes.
slos.rationale: A model is promoted into the live path only if it clears the gate — the swap is gated, not merely compared.

slos.statement: A canary that regresses under load auto-rolls-back to the last known-good model.
slos.rationale: Live qualification must be observe-and-revert: a regressing candidate is pulled automatically, not after an incident.

slos.statement: A generated document that fails its structural contract is rejected, never silently padded.
slos.rationale: Document generation consumes a validated result and repairs formatting only; a structurally-deficient deliverable fails loudly.

slos.statement: A cited report covers every verifiable claim with a resolvable citation.
slos.rationale: Citations resolve to footnotes/bibliography and unresolved markers are surfaced — every claim is cited, not 'one citation anywhere'.

slos.statement: Every generated image and audio asset carries a C2PA manifest bound to its bytes; tampering fails verification.
slos.rationale: Generated media is as auditable as text: provenance is embedded and verifiable, so an altered asset is detectable.

slos.statement: A sustained safety/quality drift signal triggers a gated rollback to the last known-good prompt version.
slos.rationale: The online loop closes itself: drift becomes a reversible, audited action, not an event into the void.

slos.statement: A candidate that regresses a previously-fixed golden case is blocked from promotion.
slos.rationale: Sequential auto-promotions can never silently undo a prior fix — the growing golden suite gates each one.

slos.statement: Online sampling and learner state persist so continual learning resumes after a restart.
slos.rationale: Continual learning must survive process restarts and aggregate across workers to be trustworthy.

slos.statement: Deep-research answers cite evidence for at least 50% of verifiable claims.
slos.rationale: Research output is cited by construction; the cited-report builder resolves and measures coverage.

slos.statement: Deep-research claims are grounded in retrieved evidence at or above a 0.5 support floor.
slos.rationale: Every claim is checked against the evidence that grounds it, not asserted free-hand.

slos.statement: The provider-backed reflector reads the actual failing cases and proposes the targeted edit the dominant failure mode calls for.
slos.rationale: GEPA-proper informed proposals beat a canned-rule floor by reflecting on real evidence.

slos.statement: Code-executing and computer-use workloads are refused on bare subprocess isolation; a real boundary (container/microVM/gVisor/WASM) is required.
slos.rationale: Process isolation is not a security boundary; adversarial workloads must run behind real isolation.

slos.statement: Computer-use actions are registered as permissioned, approval-gated tools on the audited tool runtime.
slos.rationale: GUI/browser actions ride the same RBAC + audit + approval path as any tool, not a thin adapter.

slos.statement: The provider-native computer-use hosted tool requires approval and a scoped permission.
slos.rationale: Server-executed consequential tools are governed on the same permission path as local ones.

slos.statement: The always-on egress DLP scan blocks an outbound provider request carrying credentials in block mode.
slos.rationale: Last-mile DLP is enforced at the provider boundary independent of call-site wiring, so a secret never leaves the process unflagged.

slos.statement: A signed audit chain detects a forged entry even when an attacker recomputes the public hash chain.
slos.rationale: HMAC/asymmetric per-entry signatures bind history to a key, making the chain tamper-evident against a privileged attacker.

slos.statement: Tenant-scoped retrieval returns the tenant's own and shared rows and never another tenant's, with the filter pushed into the engine.
slos.rationale: Pushing tenant scope into the backend closes the fetch-to-filter cross-tenant exfiltration risk.

slos.statement: The compiler selects image and table evidence as first-class candidates alongside text.
slos.rationale: A multimodal-native packet scores, budgets, orders, and cites images and tables in the same scored packet as text.

slos.statement: An eval metric with no reference to score against returns skipped and is excluded from gate aggregation.
slos.rationale: Unscoreable cases no longer inflate a mean to a neutral 1.0 or silently pass a gate.

slos.statement: Two distributed workers never double-execute a graph thread; the lease/CAS loser aborts.
slos.rationale: Horizontal scale is only safe if the lease + checkpoint-version CAS guarantee exactly-once super-step execution across workers.

slos.statement: A batch fanned out across the worker pool returns every result, in order, correctly.
slos.rationale: Distributed fan-out must be a transparent speedup, never a correctness regression.

slos.statement: Shared rate-limit state stays coherent: the configured per-window limit is enforced exactly.
slos.rationale: Multi-worker serving must enforce one limit, not one-per-worker; the shared store is what makes that true.

slos.statement: Quantized Matryoshka two-stage retrieval reproduces the exact index's top-1 on the bench corpus.
slos.rationale: The coarse->exact two-stage path trades memory for compute; it must not trade away recall.

slos.statement: A trained student is promoted only when it clears the significance swap gate; a regressor is blocked.
slos.rationale: The flywheel ships a cheaper model only if it provably does not regress — the same gate as a model rotation.

slos.statement: The served alert engine raises an SRE burn-rate page when the error-budget burn crosses the fast-burn threshold.
slos.rationale: A self-hosted observability plane is only useful if its alert rules actually fire on the conditions that matter.

slos.statement: The stateful-environment harness verifies task success on the database end state (not turn plausibility).
slos.rationale: End-state verification is the contract the agentic leaderboards judge on; a companion budget also gates that policy violations are rejected.

slos.statement: All nine benchmark adapters (SWE-bench/τ-bench/GAIA/WebArena/BFCL/AgentBench/ToolBench/LiveCodeBench/MMLU-Pro) replay deterministically offline.
slos.rationale: Reproducible leaderboard scores require offline determinism; a companion budget gates that each task set is hash-pinned to catch drift.

slos.statement: The retrieval-eval harness reports recall@3 of at least 0.80 on the golden set.
slos.rationale: The budget gates 0.90 to leave headroom under the published 0.80 promise.

slos.statement: A retrieval config that regresses recall/nDCG vs its recorded baseline is caught as a CI gate using the same significance test as a model swap.
slos.rationale: A re-embed or chunking change that drops recall must fail closed, not ship.

slos.statement: Every agent/server resolution across A2A, AGNTCY/ACP, and the MCP registry passes an allow-list gate and is recorded as an audited access decision.
slos.rationale: A discoverable fabric must stay governed; a companion budget gates that each resolution is recorded on the audit chain.

slos.statement: Agent and crew runs stream token/tool events that translate to a complete AG-UI run lifecycle for interactive frontends.
slos.rationale: Generative UI inherits the run's provenance/budget/audit only if it streams over the same run, not a bolt-on layer.

slos.statement: The unified self-improvement controller drives a full cycle to a gated serving decision (promote or rollback).
slos.rationale: Continual self-improvement is only safe if every cycle ends in an audited, gated promote/rollback rather than an unbounded action.

slos.statement: A prompt/policy deploy is promoted live only after clearing the canary verdict.
slos.rationale: The serving promotion surface must gate on a no-regression canary, never deploy blind.

slos.statement: Right-to-erasure produces a signed, content-bound proof that verifies against the recorded removed-id set.
slos.rationale: Erasure must be provable, not merely logged: the proof binds to exactly what was removed and is tamper-evident.

slos.statement: Tampering with an erasure proof's removed-id set is detected by the content binding.
slos.rationale: A 'we deleted everything' claim is only trustworthy if altering the recorded set breaks verification.

slos.statement: Withdrawing consent for a purpose stops the affected memories from surfacing in recall.
slos.rationale: GDPR purpose limitation must be enforced in code at recall time, not delegated to a downstream policy.

slos.statement: Per-memory ACLs admit only listed readers to team-shared memory.
slos.rationale: Team-shared memory is only safe if a non-member never recalls an ACL-restricted item.

slos.statement: As-of recall returns the value that was valid at the queried moment, including superseded facts.
slos.rationale: Bi-temporal memory must answer 'what did we believe was true at T' without mutating history.

slos.statement: The async-canonical store sustains at least 1000 concurrent saves per second on the in-memory reference path.
slos.rationale: Async-native stores must persist off the event loop without a worker-thread hop; the budget gates 1000/s, matched to the promise with wide observed headroom.

slos.statement: A live-traffic canary promotes a prompt/policy only after it clears on ramped live runs, and auto-rolls-back a regression.
slos.rationale: The live serving promotion must observe-and-revert on a regression, never deploy blind to a real-traffic ramp.

slos.statement: Every run-path persistence (interactive, streaming, and batch) goes through the async store contract, never a blocking write.
slos.rationale: Async is the canonical contract; a blocking store write on any run path would stall the event loop under load.

slos.statement: On a tool failure an agent repairs the remaining plan in place (re-binds to an alternative tool) and still finishes, rather than restarting or dead-ending.
slos.rationale: Resilience to a failing dependency is the point of in-place repair; a tool outage must not abort a run that can still succeed.

slos.statement: Under a budget shock an agent drops the optional tail of its plan and finalizes directly, returning the best answer reachable inside the budget instead of exhausting it.
slos.rationale: A budget is a hard cap; the planner must converge toward an answer under pressure, never blow the cap chasing optional work.

slos.statement: Cost-aware action selection cuts model spend by at least 25% versus always using the strongest model, by spending a cheaper capable model on the easy steps.
slos.rationale: Reaching for the strongest model on every step overpays; the floor protects the savings the registry-priced selector exists to deliver.

slos.statement: Independent sub-graphs scheduled across a worker pool achieve at least a 1.5x logical speedup over serial execution, with identical results.
slos.rationale: Independent work should run concurrently; the floor protects the speedup the work-stealing scheduler exists to deliver.

slos.statement: A graph paused on a durable timer survives a process restart and resumes when the timer comes due, without holding a worker while paused.
slos.rationale: A timer that did not survive a restart would silently never fire; durability of the wake condition is the whole guarantee.

slos.statement: An on-policy learning update is served only when it does not regress the reference policy's expected verifiable reward.
slos.rationale: Reinforcement from verifiable rewards must climb, not churn; the monotonic gate makes the served policy non-regressing in reward by construction.

slos.statement: An on-policy update stays inside its KL-to-reference trust region; the clamp holds the bound even under an aggressive step.
slos.rationale: A policy that drifts arbitrarily far from the reference is unsafe to ship; the KL clamp keeps the update inside a verifiable trust region.

slos.statement: The no-regression gate blocks a policy update that would regress the baseline reward, and the served policy never falls below it.
slos.rationale: On-policy learning must never silently trade away verifiable success; a regressing candidate is refused and the reference is served instead.
